title: Fixing the Node.js Express Middleware for Static File Serving

statement.markdown: In this task, you will be fixing a Node.js Express middleware to serve static files correctly. The middleware should serve files from the /public directory and serve index.html for any unknown routes. However, the current implementation has a logical bug.
statement.inputFormat: Input will be in the format of a URL path
statement.outputFormat: Output will be the contents of the requested file, or a 404 error message if the file is not found
statement.notes: The middleware should handle serving static files and redirecting unknown routes to index.html

examples.explanation: The middleware should serve the contents of public/style.css
The middleware should serve index.html for unknown routes

editorial.title: Editorial: Fixing the Node.js Express Middleware
editorial.intuition: The middleware should serve static files from the /public directory and serve index.html for unknown routes. Check the express.static middleware configuration to fix the logical bug.
editorial.bruteForce: One way to fix the middleware is to use the express.static middleware to serve static files and use a catch-all route to serve index.html for unknown routes.
editorial.optimalSolution: Use the express.static middleware to serve static files and use a catch-all route to serve index.html for unknown routes.
editorial.proofOfCorrectness: The middleware should serve static files and index.html correctly
commonMistakes: Forgetting to configure the express.static middleware
Using a catch-all route without serving index.html
